etymology: Nicholas = victory of the people; Brady = spirited
wordplay_note: The spirited victor who receives alien transmissions
card_description: Nicholas Brady (meaning "Nicholas = victory of the people; Brady = spirited") is a character in Philip K. Dick's *Radio Free Albemuth*. Record store clerk who receives visions from the VALIS satellite. Name note: The spirited victor who receives alien transmissions. PKD's first attempt at the VALIS narrative — the ordinary man as divine radio receiver. Appears in 4 Exegesis passages. First appears in *Radio Free Albemuth*. Context: "Dick identifies his experience as a literal fulfillment of Biblical promise (Psalm 116) and a commissioning into the class of "prophets."
